body:
## Context

Board Advisor diagnosed today (2026-05-05) why the company stopped producing backtests. Three failures stacked. Two are now fixed by Board Advisor; **the third is yours**. Full RCA: `docs/ops/COMPANY_RUNNING_FIX_2026-05-05T1820Z.md`.

**Fixed today:**
- Built `framework/scripts/p2_baseline.py` — the missing P2 runner.
- Updated Pipeline-Op AGENTS.md with explicit P2 dispatch procedure.
- Cleared `dispatch_state.json` phantom running counts.
- Smoke validating end-to-end on T1 (EURUSD.DWX H1 2024) — at 67% as of 2026-05-05T16:32Z, real trades happening.

**Not fixed — your work:**
- 700+ QUA issues, 28 in_review limbo, 5 explicit recovery-of-recovery — the cascade pattern.
- Flat issue structure with no hierarchy: agents lose context, drift triggers new issues, pipeline state is unreadable.

---

## Part 1 — Stop the cascade THIS heartbeat (~30 min)

**Operation:** mass-resolve the 28 in_review issues.

GET http://127.0.0.1:3100/api/companies/03d4dcc8-4cea-4133-9f68-90c0d99628fb/issues?status=in_review

For each:
- Verifiable work-product exists (commit landed / file present / report written) -> PATCH status=done with one closeout line citing the evidence path.
- Superseded by newer issue -> PATCH status=cancelled with comment linking to the newer.
- Genuinely waiting on someone -> PATCH blockedReason via comment, name the unblock owner.

**Hard rule, codify in your own AGENTS.md (propose to OWNER for approval):**

> When Paperclip auto-resume flips a `done` issue back to `in_progress` (state drift), DO NOT file a recovery issue. Re-PATCH the issue to `done` with a single one-line comment 'drift correction: status was already done at <commit>'. One mutation per drift, one comment, end of story. The recovery-of-recovery pattern is what filled the queue with 700+ entries.

This single rule would have prevented QUA-714, QUA-732, QUA-733, QUA-735, QUA-736.

---

## Part 2 — Design Research-Run hierarchy (this week)

OWNER 2026-05-05 proposes structural fix: replace flat QUA issue model with hierarchy that matches `PIPELINE_PHASE_SPEC.md` and Strategy Card model.

**Proposed shape:**

```
QUA-NNN-PARENT: Research Run 2026-05 (active EAs: 1003, 1004, 1009, 1017)
  +- QUA-NNN-1003: QM5_1003 davey_baseline_3bar - full pipeline G0..P10
  |   +- QUA-662 (existing): P2 baseline screening   <- currently in_progress
  |   +- QUA-NEW: P3 parameter sweep (gated on P2 PASS)
  |   +- QUA-NEW: P3.5 cross-sectional robustness
  |   +- ... through P10
  +- QUA-NNN-1004: QM5_1004 davey-es-breakout (P1 build)
  +- QUA-NNN-1009: QM5_SRC04_S03 lien-fade-double-zeros (P1 build)
  +- QUA-NNN-1017: QM5_1017 chan-pairs-stat-arb (G0 card unblock)
```

**Why this is structurally better:**
- Stops cascade at root: drift visible at parent, no need for recovery issues.
- Matches docs: PIPELINE_PHASE_SPEC defines G0..P10 as a sequence; hierarchy mirrors spec.
- Aligns with Kanban: `paperclip/kanban/company_kanban.csv` already has process+phase columns expressing this.
- Aligns with Strategy Cards: one card = one EA = one parent-child branch.
- Pipeline-Op picks children of active parent -> no more lost work.

**Migration approach (gradual, NOT big-bang):**

1. Define issue-template schema for parent/child/grandchild (description format, required fields). Owner: CEO.
2. Create the `Research Run 2026-05` parent issue with the 4 active EAs as children, existing in-flight phase issues as grandchildren. Owner: CEO.
3. New EAs only use new structure. Owner: All agents.
4. Drain old flat issues via Part 1 mass-resolve. Owner: CEO.
5. Migrate already-active EAs (e.g. QM5_1003) by re-parenting QUA-662 under the new hierarchy. Owner: CEO.

**Update agent prompts:**
- Pipeline-Op: 'pick children of currently-active Research Run parent before considering anything else'
- CEO: 'each heartbeat, check parent issue health; close completed children; promote next phase children'
- CTO: 'reviews land as comments on the EA child issue, not as separate review issues'

---

## Acceptance criteria

You are done with QUA-737 when:

- Active in_review count drops from 28 to 5 or less.
- You posted the no-recovery-issue rule in a comment on this issue (OWNER will approve or reject in next review cycle).
- You created the parent `Research Run 2026-05` issue with 4 children for the 4 active EAs.
- You re-parented QUA-662 under the QM5_1003 child.
- You drafted the issue-template schema in `processes/research_run_hierarchy.md` (commit on agents/ceo branch).
- You closed this issue with a summary comment listing the in_review count before/after, the no-recovery rule final wording, and a link to the new hierarchy parent.

---

## What is explicitly NOT your job

- DO NOT touch `framework/scripts/p2_baseline.py` - Board Advisor owns that.
- DO NOT pause/unpause agents - OWNER-class.
- DO NOT create recovery issues for any drift you encounter while doing this cleanup. If you observe drift, just re-PATCH and move on.
- DO NOT re-open QUA-731, QUA-733, QUA-684 - all done, evidence in commits 1dff5e0c, 680a2167, 54814568.

---

## Priority

Part 1: this heartbeat. Part 2: this week.

Pipeline can run again with current flat structure (Board Advisor wired the P2 runner). But token-burn rate doubles every day the cascade pattern stays unaddressed; hierarchy makes that durable instead of bandaid.

Go. Two heartbeats from now we should be back to producing real V5 evidence.

- Board Advisor (local-board), 2026-05-05T16:35Z
